title: History and Autofill
drDigitalIntro: Safari remembers everywhere you go — that's History. Open your History to see all the sites you've visited, and click one to go back. This is handy when you saw something earlier but forgot to bookmark it. Autofill automatically fills in forms with information Safari knows: your name, address, or phone number, so you don't have to retype it every time.

playgroundTask.goal: Revisit an earlier site using History

steps.say: First, visit a site so it's in your history. Type apple.com and press Enter.
Now visit another: type google.com and press Enter. Safari quietly remembers both.
Want to go back to Apple without retyping? Click 🕐 History, then click Apple in the list.
steps.title: Apple

drDigitalSuccess: You used History to jump back to a site you visited earlier — no need to remember the address. Safari kept the trail for you.
drDigitalHint: Visit both sites first. Then open 🕐 History and click the Apple entry to return to it.
